result:
Done. Workspaces now use a full role-based permission system, verified end-to-end against the local database (typecheck clean, all smoke tests passed, test data cleaned up). Changes are left uncommitted for your review.

## How it works

**Data model** (`migrations/003_roles.sql`): three new tables â€” `roles` (per-workspace, case-insensitive unique names), `role_permissions`, and `member_roles` (many-to-many, so members hold multiple roles). Composite foreign keys guarantee a role can only ever be assigned within its own workspace. The migration seeds `ADMIN`/`GUEST` for existing workspaces, maps old `owner` â†’ ADMIN and `member` â†’ GUEST, then drops the old single-role column.

**Permissions** (`src/workspaces/permissions.ts`): three permissions â€” `members.read`, `members.invite`, `roles.manage`. A `requireWorkspacePermission(...)` middleware guards every workspace-scoped route: 404 for non-members (same response as a nonexistent workspace, as the existing code did), 403 for members lacking the permission. ADMIN's permissions are implicit-all in one query rather than stored, so roles added in future releases can never leave admins behind; its permission set is not editable, and the last ADMIN holder can't have the role removed â€” a workspace can't lock itself out. GUEST starts with `members.read` and is editable like any custom role.

**New endpoints** (`src/workspaces/roles.ts`), all under `/workspaces/:id`:
- `GET /roles` â€” list roles with permissions (`members.read`)
- `POST /roles` â€” create a custom role with an optional permission set; 409 on duplicate name (`roles.manage`)
- `PUT /roles/:roleId/permissions` â€” replace a role's permissions transactionally (`roles.manage`)
- `POST /members/:userId/roles` â€” assign; idempotent (201 created / 200 already held)
- `DELETE /members/:userId/roles/:roleId` â€” remove; the last-admin check runs under a row lock on the role so concurrent removals can't race past it

**Existing routes updated**: workspace creation grants the creator ADMIN in the same transaction; invitation accept grants GUEST only to genuinely new members; inviting requires `members.invite` instead of "owner"; member/workspace listings now return a `roles` array instead of the single `role` field (a small response-shape change for API clients).

The smoke test covered the full lifecycle: inviteâ†’accept, guest denied inviting (403) then allowed after being granted a custom "Recruiter" role, denied again after that role's permissions were stripped, last-admin removal rejected (409), admin handoff (promote then self-demote), cross-workspace role assignment rejected, and outsiders getting 404s throughout.
